title: Siphoneranthemum senense

It belongs to the genus Siphoneranthemum, which is a group of bromeliads native to the Americas. The plant is further classified as a member of the order Poales, a large and diverse group of monocotyledonous flowering plants. Within the Bromeliaceae family, Siphoneranthemum senense is part of the subfamily Tillandsioideae. intro: Siphoneranthemum senense, also known as the Cape daisy or Cape chamomile, is a species of flowering plant native to South Africa, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including insomnia, anxiety, and digestive problems, due to its calming and soothing effects.
